title: Write API reference docs for a rate limiter class

messages.content: Write accurate, concise API documentation in Markdown. Do not invent methods, attributes, or behavior that are not in the code. Use the exact parameter names and types from the code.

Document the following Python class as API reference docs (Markdown). Include a one-line summary, the constructor signature with parameter types/defaults, and each method with signature, parameters, return type, and a one-line description. Also note any errors raised.

```python
class TokenBucket:
    """Thread-safe token bucket rate limiter."""

    def __init__(self, capacity: int, refill_rate: float, burst: int | None = None):
        """Create a bucket.

        capacity: max tokens the bucket holds.
        refill_rate: tokens added per second.
        burst: optional max tokens withdrawable in one call (defaults to capacity).
        """
        self.capacity = capacity
        self.refill_rate = refill_rate
        self.burst = burst if burst is not None else capacity
        self._tokens = float(capacity)
        self._last = time.monotonic()
        self._lock = threading.Lock()

    def acquire(self, tokens: int = 1, timeout: float | None = None) -> bool:
        """Try to withdraw tokens.

        If timeout is None, return False immediately when tokens are insufficient.
        If timeout is a number, block up to that many seconds waiting for refill.
        Returns True if the tokens were granted, False otherwise.
        """
        ...

    def remaining(self) -> float:
        """Return the currently available tokens (may be fractional)."""
        ...
```

Output the Markdown only — no preamble, no closing remarks.

metadata.mainstream_gap: Models frequently hallucinate methods (release, try_acquire) and invent async variants when documenting rate limiters.

title: Write a README that matches a project directory tree

messages.content: Write a README.md in Markdown. Base it ONLY on the information given. Do not invent features, dependencies, or commands that are not present in the project structure.

Write a README.md for this project based on the directory tree and file descriptions below. Include: project title, one-paragraph description, features (from the listed modules), installation, and usage examples (from the listed scripts). Do not add anything not supported by the tree.

```
myproj/
  pyproject.toml          # package: myproj, requires-python >= 3.11, deps: [httpx, pydantic]
  README.md
  myproj/
    __init__.py
    client.py             # Client: async HTTP client wrapping httpx
    models.py             # pydantic models: Request, Response, Error
    retry.py              # retry decorator with exponential backoff
  scripts/
    fetch.py              # CLI: python -m myproj.scripts.fetch <url>
  tests/
    test_client.py
    test_retry.py
```

Output the Markdown only.

metadata.mainstream_gap: Models pad READMEs with unsupported install commands, Docker sections, and framework mentions not in the project.

title: Write a Keep-a-Changelog entry from commit messages

messages.content: Write a Keep a Changelog (https://keepachangelog.com) style entry. Group commits into Added / Changed / Fixed / Removed. Use imperative mood, one line per change. Do not invent changes.

Write the Unreleased section of a CHANGELOG.md from these commits:

- feat: add CSV export to reports
- fix: correct token cost calculation for Gemini
- docs: update adapter guide for command agent
- refactor: split stats module into stats and reporting
- feat: add --repeat flag for pass@k sampling
- fix: handle empty case list in list command

Output the Markdown section only (start with ## [Unreleased]).

metadata.mainstream_gap: Models often mis-categorize commits (e.g., putting a fix under Changed) or add empty sections.

title: Write a user guide section from a CLI spec

messages.content: Write a concise user guide section in Markdown. Cover only the flags and behavior described. Give concrete command examples.

Write a user guide section for the 'ckl run' command based on this spec:

- Usage: ckl run [TARGET] [CASE_SET] [options]
- TARGET: mock (default), openai:MODEL, anthropic:MODEL, gemini:MODEL, command, or a registered namespace like deepseekv4
- CASE_SET: all (default), chat, agent, doc-writing, infra-code, paper-reading, or a path to JSONL files
- Options:
  - --repeat N        run each case N times for pass@k / pass^k
  - --concurrency N   run N cases in parallel (default 1)
  - --cache           enable response cache (chat cases only)
  - --judge TARGET    use a model as judge for semantic checks
  - --fail-under X    exit non-zero if score is below X (CI gate)
  - --run-name NAME   use a stable output directory name

Include at least four realistic examples. Output Markdown only.

metadata.mainstream_gap: Models often omit less-common flags or invent options not in the spec when writing CLI documentation.
